Contest for the Indo-Pacific

07 Jul 2020

Getting introduced to this book

The border is tense. The Ladakh known for its snowy cold climate is hotly discussed by the media. The reason being Chinese aggressive intrusion across LAC, amidst the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic. ThePrint.in’s Cut the clutter is so good in clearing the muddled political events. This book was introduced through one of its video.

What’s the book is about?

It’s a nonfiction book on Indo-Pacific, an idea or a geopolitical region, written by Rory Medcalf. The author is a Professor has been Head of the National Security College (NSC) at the Australian National University.

Contents

The book’s full title is Contest for the Indo-Pacific: Why China Won’t Map the Future. So, obviously this book revolves around the nation.

CHAPTER 1. Of Names, Maps and Power This chapter details us about how did the term Indo-Pacific came into existence. Who coined the term, who are all the stack-holders of the region and significance of the region.

CHAPTER 2. A Submerged History of Asia This chapter travels across the time to tell us who was the leader of the region from starting of this millennium to how the European saw the region, how the 2 great wars shaped the nations and how the China behaves very similar to the European colonisers. It touches the empires - Chola, Srivijaya, Ming Dynasty, General - Zheng He, philosophers - Chanakya, Sun Tzu, Rabindranath Tagore.

CHAPTER 3. Odyssey of Nations: The Quest for a Regional Home This chapter covered the importance of Asian unity thorough Asian Relations Conference by listing the representatives from various regions of Asia. There is a quote mentioned in this chapter,

I had a selfish motive in wanting India to emerge… If India doesn’t emerge, Asia will be submerged - Lee Kuan Yew, Singapore’s founding leader

The chapter ends with the formation of ASEAN + Six bringing India, Australia & New Zealand into the sphere.

PS: This will be updated along with reading the book
